consequence_relation.name: abstract selfextensional consequence relation
consequence_relation.notes: The Lean slice keeps consequence abstract and records selfextensionality as an explicit imported metalogical property.

metalogical_properties.description: Interderivability is treated as the equivalence relation available to normative definitions.
Closure is represented as the set of formulas entailed by a premise set.
A Boolean consequence relation is verified as a selfextensional instance in Lean.
A three-valued preorder consequence relation is verified as a nonclassical selfextensional instance in Lean.

normative_systems.description: A normative system is represented as a relation from input formulas to output formulas.
The first output operation derives consequences from directly triggered norms.

permission_families.description: Permission by compatibility with all obligations under the same condition, with Propositions 2.6 and 4.2-4.4 represented abstractly.
Permission by absence of obligation for the negated formula.
Static permission requires the full normative extension criterion.
Dynamic permission requires the full update criterion.

formalization_targets.reason: Represented as a parameterized Lean theorem: under weakening of output, inconsistency-to-contrary entailment, and contrary inconsistency, conditional negative permission is the largest compatible permission system.

Represented as a parameterized Lean theorem equating the generalized compatibility definition of negative permission with absence of contrary obligation under weakening of output and negation/consistency assumptions.
Represented as a Lean theorem: internal coherence entails almost inclusion of a normative system in its generalized negative permission system.
Represented as a Lean theorem: inclusion of normative systems is antitone for generalized negative permission.
